title: Author ShopHomeView — the storefront screen
description: Build the stateless storefront view for the Turbo Widgets Shop. This is the first screen the visitor sees: a TCollectionHeader hero, a TCategorySection listing the five aisles, and a 'Featured' TCollectionSection showing every widget in the catalog with a working TCollectionToolbar above it.

context.layout_anatomy: Top: TCollectionHeader with title 'Turbo Widgets Shop' and a short tagline. Middle: TCategorySection with one TCategoryCard per aisle (tap opens that aisle detail). Bottom: TCollectionSection 'Featured' rendered in a bento layout by default, listing every widget via TCollectionCard. Above Featured: a TCollectionToolbar bound to the view model's search/sort/filter/layout cells, actually filtering the list as you type.
context.card_visual_rule: Per the spec, visual widgets render a tiny live miniature of themselves inside the TCollectionCard on listings. Structural/non-visual widgets (TContextualButtons, ContextualButtonsProvider, TViewBuilder, TResponsiveBuilder) fall back to a LucideIcon + name + tagline layout. Both paths render inside TCollectionCard so the listing is visually uniform.
context.view_pattern: Stateless TViewBuilder<ShopHomeViewModel>. All state is read from the view model via ValueListenableBuilder / ValueListenableBuilderX2..X6 / MultiListenableBuilder. Empty state (when search filters to zero results) renders an in-place message, not an error.
context.tap_behaviour: Tapping a category card routes to that aisle detail. Tapping a featured card opens that widget's product detail. Navigation is emitted via the view model's callback, handled by the shell.

goals: example/lib/showcase/feature/shop_home/views/shop_home/shop_home_view.dart exists as a StatelessWidget wrapping a TViewBuilder<ShopHomeViewModel>.
The view composes: TCollectionHeader hero → TCategorySection of aisles → TCollectionToolbar → TCollectionSection 'Featured'.
The toolbar mutates the featured list live on every keystroke / option change.
Empty search results render an in-place empty state (no error chrome).
Visual widgets in the featured list render mini-previews; structural widgets fall back to the Lucide icon treatment — and both variants render inside TCollectionCard.
Every callback flows through the view model — no setState, no local GlobalKey state.
non_goals: Any business logic — no filter/sort/derivation in the view.
Pushing the per-route contextual buttons config (owned by the shell integration task).
Building any product detail body — just the card surfaces.
acceptance_criteria: Launching the app and landing on shop home shows the hero, the five aisle cards, and the featured list.
Typing in the toolbar search narrows the featured list live.
Changing the toolbar sort/filter/layout options changes the ordering/visual of the featured list live.
Clearing all filters restores the full featured list.
Tapping an aisle card routes to that aisle detail; tapping a featured card opens the product detail.
Empty search results show an in-place empty state.
No Material AppBar/Drawer/BottomNavigationBar is used anywhere in this view.
constraints: Stateless view. No setState. No local state hooks. All state flows from the view model.
No Material chrome widgets. Use TCollectionHeader, TCategorySection, TCategoryCard, TCollectionSection, TCollectionCard, TCollectionToolbar exclusively.
tools_to_use: RepoPrompt MCP to read TCollectionHeader, TCategorySection, TCategoryCard, TCollectionSection, TCollectionCard, TCollectionToolbar sources.

testing_and_verification.unit_tests: None — stateless ui. Verification is manual.
verification_gate: Manual verification of every acceptance criterion in a running app.
Use dart-mcp get_widget_tree to confirm the tree contains the expected turbo_widgets types and no Material chrome.

quality_checklist.maintainability: Adding a new aisle requires no edits to this view — the category section reads from the catalog service through the view model.
quality_checklist.dry: No duplication of card-building logic. Extract a small private helper if both the visual-miniature path and the icon-fallback path share structure.
quality_checklist.single_responsibility: This view renders shop home. It does not route, it emits route intent.
quality_checklist.performance: Featured list rebuilds only on filter/sort/layout changes, not on every shell state change.

workflow_instructions: Add `follow_up_notes` to 19 with the contextual buttons config this screen expects.
follow_up_notes: Phase 3 task 04 created the ShopHomeViewModel. Public API:
Constructor: ShopHomeViewModel({required ShowcaseCatalogService, void Function(ShowcaseRoute)? onProductSelected})
State (ValueListenable getters): searchQuery (String), sortValue (String?), filterValue (String?), featuredLayout (TCollectionSectionLayout)
Mutators: updateSearchQuery(String), updateSortValue(String?), updateFilterValue(String?), updateLayout(TCollectionSectionLayout), selectProduct(ShowcaseRoute)
Not registered in GetIt — construct inline in TViewBuilder, passing onProductSelected callback from shell context
The view should use MultiListenableBuilder over searchQuery+sortValue+filterValue+featuredLayout, then call featuredEntries inside the builder
